- Record high-value future work such as spaced repetition, audio, analytics, richer CMS capabilities, and learner note-taking.
- Keep these issues blocked behind MVP completion unless the plan changes intentionally.
- Preserve the reasoning for deferral so future selves do not have to reconstruct it from scratch.
acceptance_criteria:
- Major non-MVP ideas are represented explicitly.
- Each item explains why it matters and why it is deferred.
- The epic remains clearly downstream of MVP readiness.
notes:
- Scope discipline is part of delivery quality.
- If one of these items becomes urgent, update the plan and graph intentionally rather than sneaking it in.
- The backlog should help the team say not yet with clarity.

title: Post-MVP: Add a spaced-repetition engine on top of vocabulary progress
description:
Build a real spaced-repetition system once the baseline vocabulary flow is stable.

The plan leaves room for this explicitly, but it is intentionally deferred until deterministic session and progress foundations are proven.
design:
- Design review scheduling around stable progress data rather than replacing the MVP flow prematurely.
- Reuse the vocabulary progress/session foundations instead of bypassing them.
- Decide how SRS interacts with repeat mode, next-review timestamps, and learner UX.
acceptance_criteria:
- A concrete SRS design and implementation path exists.
- The feature builds on stable MVP vocabulary behavior.
- Scope and UX remain understandable rather than exploding in complexity.
notes:
- This is intentionally post-MVP because foundational session correctness matters more first.
- Future work should start from this issue rather than reopening the same design debate.
- Do not let SRS pressure destabilize the simpler MVP loop.

title: Post-MVP: Add audio pronunciation and richer media asset handling
description:
Add audio pronunciation and richer managed media support once the core learning and content workflows are stable.

The plan keeps this in view but outside the initial MVP.
design:
- Extend asset handling, storage, and frontend playback for first-class audio.
- Reuse the storage abstraction rather than inventing a separate media path.
- Decide how audio fits vocabulary and potentially reading surfaces.
acceptance_criteria:
- There is a coherent implementation path for pronunciation/media support.
- The feature fits the existing storage and delivery model.
- Audio does not destabilize core content and learner flows.
notes:
- This is deferred to protect MVP focus.
- Media is valuable, but the system should first prove its content and learner reliability loop.
- Future work should leverage the earlier artifact/storage decisions.

title: Post-MVP: Add learner analytics and product-performance surfaces
description:
Build richer learner and product analytics surfaces once core flows are operational and the team is ready to interpret the signals responsibly.

Stable product behavior precedes meaningful analytics.
design:
- Extend the observability baseline into product-facing analytics and dashboards.
- Decide which metrics belong in admin/product surfaces versus internal ops tooling.
- Keep the analytics model grounded in real user questions, not vanity counters.
acceptance_criteria:
- The project has a clear path for analytics after MVP.
- Analytics design builds on the existing observability work instead of duplicating it.
- The resulting surfaces support product decisions, not just data accumulation.
notes:
- Use the backlog to capture intent now without distracting MVP delivery.
- Future selves should be able to start here with context already preserved.
- Do not confuse richer metrics with first-order delivery work.

title: Post-MVP: Evolve the admin experience from import workflows toward a richer CMS
description:
Expand the admin surface from the MVP's import/preview/publish workflows into a richer CMS experience once the core operational loop has proven itself.

This is a classic source of scope creep, so capture it explicitly and keep it downstream.
design:
- Decide which editing and content-management capabilities belong in-product after MVP.
- Preserve the existing publication and audit model instead of bypassing it for convenience.
- Keep operator trust and change visibility central.
acceptance_criteria:
- There is a concrete path for richer admin tooling after MVP.
- New CMS capabilities fit the revision/publication safety model.
- Scope growth remains intentional rather than accidental.
notes:
- The MVP admin goal is operational import/publish, not a full CMS.
- Defer until the baseline loop is solid and the real pain points are known.
- Use this issue to keep that boundary explicit.

title: Post-MVP: Add learner notes, bookmarks, and personal study affordances
description:
Add personal learner-side affordances such as notes and bookmarks after the core study flows are stable.

These features are useful, but they are not part of the MVP's primary reliability-first loop.
design:
- Decide which personal annotations are most valuable and how they fit Reading/Grammar/Vocabulary surfaces.
- Preserve clean separation from core progress semantics.
- Keep the initial design intentionally small and user-centered.
acceptance_criteria:
- There is a clear post-MVP backlog item for personal study features.
- The work can build on stable learner modules instead of competing with them.
- Scope remains additive rather than destabilizing.
notes:
- This is valuable product work, just not first-loop work.
- Future selves should revisit it once real usage reveals which personal tools matter most.
- Avoid pulling this into MVP just because it sounds user-friendly.
